_source: Curated descriptions distilled from the BESSER-PEARL/User-Modeling-Language metamodel component READMEs (PersonalInformation, Accessibility, Competence, Culture) at https://github.com/BESSER-PEARL/User-Modeling-Language/tree/main/metamodel. The bundled user_metamodel.json carries no descriptive text of its own, so these are used to ground natural-language explanations of the user-profile elements. Only the classes present in the bundled short metamodel are covered.
overview: A User Profile describes a *target user* (a group of users, not one individual) as a set of class-instance boxes drawn from a fixed metamodel. Every box attaches, directly or through a grouping element, to a single root User element. Each attribute row is a matching CRITERION with a comparison operator (e.g. "age >= 18", "level == B2"), not a fixed value.

classes.User: The root element of a user profile. It carries no attributes of its own; it anchors the profile, and every other element attaches to it directly or through a grouping element (Personal_Information, Culture, Competence, Accessibility).
classes.Personal_Information: Demographic identity data — the kind of information a user would enter on a profile page of a social-media platform or similar to describe who they are (name, age, nationality, address, gender). Attaches directly to User (at most one per profile).
classes.Culture: The user's cultural background — social behaviour, institutions and norms, typically tied to a region. Its dimensions follow Hofstede's cultural model, and it also records the user's religion. Attaches directly to User (at most one per profile).
classes.Competence: A grouping element for how capable the user is: it bundles the user's languages, skills and education. It carries no attributes itself — the concrete competences live in the Language, Skill and Education boxes attached to it. Attaches directly to User (at most one per profile).
classes.Accessibility: A grouping element for the user's accessibility needs — everything relating to how sensory, motor or cognitive capabilities affect their interaction. It carries no attributes itself; concrete needs are expressed through the Disability boxes attached to it. Attaches directly to User (at most one per profile).
classes.Language: A language the user speaks, recorded as an ISO 639-3 code with proficiency on the CEFR scale. Attaches under Competence; a profile may have several Language boxes.
classes.Skill: The user's proficiency in performing specific tasks or actions. Attaches under Competence; a profile may have several Skill boxes.
classes.Education: A degree or diploma the user holds, together with its field of study and awarding institution. Attaches under Competence; a profile may have several Education boxes.
classes.Disability: A concrete disability the user might have and the aspect(s) of life or interaction it affects (e.g. Sight, Hearing, Mobility). Attaches under Accessibility; a profile may have several Disability boxes.

attributes.Personal_Information.firstName: The user's given name. Uniquely identifies one individual, so it is usually left blank for a target-user profile.
attributes.Personal_Information.lastName: The user's family name. Uniquely identifies one individual, so it is usually left blank for a target-user profile.
attributes.Personal_Information.nationality_iso3166: The user's nationality, as an ISO 3166 country code.
attributes.Personal_Information.address: Where the user lives. Uniquely identifies one individual, so it is usually left blank for a target-user profile.
attributes.Personal_Information.gender: The gender the user identifies with.
attributes.Language.iso693_3: The language, given as an ISO 639-3 three-letter code.
attributes.Language.level: The user's proficiency in the language, on the CEFR scale (A1 lowest to C2 highest).
attributes.Skill.name: The skill in question.
attributes.Skill.score: How proficient the user is in the skill.
attributes.Education.fieldOfDegree: The field of study of the degree.
attributes.Education.degreeName: The name of the degree or diploma.
attributes.Education.degreeType: The kind of degree (e.g. Bachelor's, Master's, Doctorate).
attributes.Education.providedBy: The institution that awarded the degree.
attributes.Disability.name: The name of the disability.
attributes.Disability.description: A short description of the disability.
attributes.Disability.affects: The aspect of life or interaction the disability affects (e.g. Sight, Hearing, Mobility, Cognitive).
